creator: ino-news@spotteswoode.dnsalias.org
text:
this is freebsd 7.0-current on a gigabyte "M61P-S3" motherboard. the
CPU is an "AMD Athlon(tm) 64 X2 Dual Core Processor 4200+ (2210.04-MHz
K8-class CPU)", socket AM2. the chipset is quite similar or even
identical to nVidias nForce MCP61. the handbook states: "nVidia GeForce
6100/nForce 430". the BIOS is "AWARD", it lets you boot from USB, is
flashable and so on.

audio:  Realtek "ALC883" hi-def -> works using "kldload snd_hda"
ethernet:  Realtek "RTL8211" gigabit -> use "kldload if_nfe".
  i've yet to get the router up on the nfe0 interface, in the meantime
  i'm using some PCI-card.  networking is net-working, absolutely no
  problems.
disk:  SATA300 works.
x11:  using "xorg-7.3_1" with (builtin) "nv" driver.
  ports/x11/nvidia-driver doesn't build on amd64, i don't care.
USB:  10xUSB 1.1/2.0 -> work using "kldload umass" or whatever you need.

i didn't test the parallel, serial and the firewire ports yet.  i wanted
them badly to be able to do embedded development using dongles.  both
BIOS

to get SMBus working i needed the patch from kern/108830 (hint, hint).
as this chipset only needs a few PCI-IDs added here and there, people
won't need to do any patching in the (near?) future, me hopes.

freebsd-7 using amd64, which has no official release yet(!), is working
rock-solid for me. this is going to be a very nice piece of hardware/
software combination. i used the AMD64 snapshot to boot and later
removed options "WITNESS" and "GDB" in favour of "SCHED_ULE" and "KTR",
"ALQ", "KTR_ALQ". i also removed most of the drivers available as kernel
modules.

regards, clemens

here's the dmesg(1):
